head: 955ec2f
handoff_notice: HARD RULE (CLAUDE.md): Claude and Codex must never commit to the same branch. claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 was created at the user's explicit instruction by branching from Codex's codex/go-live-v1.6-phase7 at the same tip commit (b67acfd, no content change) and is now Claude-owned and pushed to origin. Codex's original branch/worktree (/Users/aneenaananth/gsd-workspaces/codex-go-live-phase7/edgartools-platform) was left untouched, not deleted, not rebased. All further commits must go to claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7, not the codex branch.

completed_tasks.name: Production AWS Infrastructure (passive) + edgar-identity secret — terraform apply (42 added, 0 destroyed), versions.tf fix, edgar-identity secret populated, human-approved at checkpoint.
Active Application Deploy — :dev->:prod ECR re-tag, deploy-aws-application.sh --env prod (exit 0, 22 state machines, 5 ECS task defs), non-secret evidence + matrix flips.
Phase 06 goal verification against live AWS state.
completed_tasks.result: PASSED, 10/10 must-haves (after fixing two doc-accuracy gaps a first verifier pass found)

completed_tasks.name: Production native-pull preflight (Codex-authored). Stopped before the approval checkpoint: all six prod operator-local Terraform input files are absent. Recorded SNOW-03 as BLOCKED with secret-safe evidence.
Production dbt/gold dependency check (Codex-authored). Stopped at Task 1: 07-02 requires 07-01 to pass first. Recorded SNOW-04 as dependency-BLOCKED with secret-safe evidence.

Real production native-pull apply (branch takeover continuation). Operator supplied ACCOUNTADMIN access; created the 6 local input files; fixed 6 versions.tf constraints, a bad tfvars.example default, imported 3 shared IAM roles, namespaced 3 inline policies, switched auth to password, resolved a dashboard-object ordering race. All 3 Terraform roots applied with zero destroys. native_pull_ready=true.

Created EDGARTOOLS_PROD_DEPLOYER Snowflake user, granted the role, verified end-to-end (auth + live SELECT on source tables), stored credentials in new secret edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake. Rotated once after an internal scripting bug briefly exposed a since-invalidated password in tool output (see TODOS.md).
completed_tasks.result: SNOW-04 dependency cleared; dbt deps/run/test deliberately not run

completed_tasks.name: Ran dbt deps/run/test --target prod using edgartools-prod/dbt/snowflake credentials. 15 of 16 models passed immediately; FINANCIAL_FACTS failed on a genuine pre-existing schema-drift bug (live SEC_FINANCIAL_FACT table missing PERIOD_START, also present in dev). Fixed with a non-destructive 3-step ALTER TABLE migration against prod only. Retry: 16/16 models built, 47/47 tests pass. Captured EDGARTOOLS_GOLD_STATUS (no rows, expected) and dynamic-table freshness (all 15 ACTIVE) evidence.

remaining_tasks.name: Execute MDM secrets/connectivity, hosted graph E2E, dashboard UAT, and final GO decision phases in roadmap order.

blockers.description: Production MDM Secrets Manager values are not populated for edgartools-prod/mdm/postgres_dsn, edgartools-prod/mdm/neo4j, edgartools-prod/mdm/api_keys, edgartools-prod/mdm/snowflake. The 4 empty containers exist (created by Phase 6's terraform apply) but carry no AWSCURRENT version.
blockers.workaround: Owned by Phase 8 / MDM-02, explicitly deferred (D-05/D-06) — not in scope for Phase 6 or 7.

blockers.description: Prod hosted graph E2E has not yet passed against production Snowflake, MDM secrets, and Native App compute pool.
blockers.workaround: Phase 9 follows Snowflake/dbt readiness and MDM database readiness.

blockers.description: Prod dashboard UAT has not yet run against production or production-like read-only configuration.
blockers.workaround: Phase 10 follows CLI, dbt, and Native App gates.

blockers.description: Row 13 of the launch gate matrix (dev->prod bronze data sync) stays BLOCKED — the prod bronze bucket now exists (Phase 6 prerequisite satisfied) but the documented S3-to-S3 sync itself has not been run; explicitly out of Phase 6 scope.
blockers.workaround: Run the documented sync (01-LAUNCH-GATE-MATRIX.md row 13) whenever bronze data parity with dev is needed; no phase currently owns this as a hard gate.

human_actions_pending.action: Apply the identical PERIOD_START migration to EDGARTOOLS_DEV.EDGARTOOLS_SOURCE.SEC_FINANCIAL_FACT before ever running dbt run --target dev --select financial_facts.
human_actions_pending.context: Confirmed dev has the identical schema-drift gap as prod did; intentionally not fixed in this prod-only continuation.

human_actions_pending.action: Consider whether the dev/prod shared, non-namespaced IAM roles in runtime_access module (TODOS.md) warrant a coordinated fix across both environments.
human_actions_pending.context: Not blocking Phase 7; the 3 inline policies were namespaced to stop cross-environment secret overwrite, but the 3 roles themselves remain shared by design until a coordinated re-pointing of both envs' ECS task defs is done.
Security enforcement is on for this workstream and no 06-SECURITY.md exists yet. Advisory — does not block Phase 7 retry, but should be closed before the final v1.6 go/no-go audit.

human_actions_pending.action: Later Snowflake, MDM, dashboard reviewer, release owner, and security/isolation approvals as required by Phases 7-11.
human_actions_pending.context: The v1.6 roadmap requires owner sign-off before the go/no-go packet can flip to GO.

decisions.decision: User explicitly instructed taking over codex/go-live-v1.6-phase7 ("take over branch codex/go-live-v1.6-phase7"). Re-rooted as claude/go-live-v1.6-phase7 from the same tip commit (b67acfd) in the Claude go-live worktree, pushed to origin.
decisions.rationale: CLAUDE.md HARD RULE permits taking over the other runtime's branch only with explicit user instruction; Codex's original branch/worktree was left untouched rather than deleted or rebased.

decisions.decision: Plans 07-01 and 07-02 (both Codex-authored, inherited as-is) correctly stopped at secret-safe BLOCKED evidence rather than fabricating prod credentials or running dbt against an unconfirmed source layer.
decisions.rationale: D-05/D-06/D-10 discipline — no plan should synthesize placeholder secrets or skip a dependency gate to make progress look real.

decisions.decision: Phase 6 fixes the versions.tf constraint bug directly as an ISO-01 exception (same pattern as Phase 4's dashboard README rewrite).
decisions.rationale: Hard blocker for the real apply this phase exists to run; fixing in-phase avoids a separate non-go-live commit.

decisions.decision: Only edgartools-prod-edgar-identity receives a put-secret-value call; the other 5 secret containers (4 MDM + runner_credentials) stay empty shells.
decisions.rationale: D-05/D-06 — MDM secret values are explicitly Phase 8/MDM-02 scope, not Phase 6.

decisions.decision: terraform apply uses the saved plan file (apply tfplan), never a fresh re-plan.
decisions.rationale: D-04 — zero drift between human approval and execution for the project's first real prod apply.

decisions.decision: infra/aws-prod-application.json is generated but deliberately never staged/committed; only a non-secret summary (file presence, top-level keys, state-machine names, digest-vs-tag format) is recorded in evidence/aws.md.
decisions.rationale: D-10 / Generated-JSON Summary Rule — the manifest contains ARNs and resource identifiers that must not be committed.

uncommitted_files: infra/aws-prod-application.json (intentionally untracked — generated deploy manifest, never to be committed per D-10)
next_action: Phase 7's Snowflake gates (SNOW-03, SNOW-04) are both PASS. Next is Phase 8 (MDM secrets/connectivity).
context_notes: Phase 6 is fully complete and verified (10/10 must-haves). Codex independently scoped/planned/executed Phase 7 on codex/go-live-v1.6-phase7, reaching two BLOCKED-with-evidence outcomes (SNOW-03/SNOW-04) for the correct reason: missing prod Snowflake credentials. At the user's explicit instruction this branch was taken over by Claude. SNOW-03 passed via a real production terraform apply across all 3 Snowflake-side Terraform roots. SNOW-04 then required running dbt deps/run/test --target prod, which surfaced a genuine pre-existing schema-drift bug: the live SEC_FINANCIAL_FACT table (in both prod and dev) was missing a PERIOD_START column the dbt model, the Python silver parser, and the Python serving schema all already expected. The fix already existed in the checked-in bootstrap SQL as an ALTER TABLE migration statement but had never been (re-)executed against either live database. Applied a non-destructive 3-step migration (ADD COLUMN nullable -> backfill UPDATE, 0 rows affected since table was empty -> SET NOT NULL) against prod only; dev intentionally left with the identical gap, out of scope for this prod-only task. Re-ran dbt: 16/16 gold models built, 47/47 tests pass. SNOW-04 now PASSES. A password was briefly exposed in agent tool output earlier in this phase due to a Python quoting bug; it was immediately rotated before being stored anywhere (see TODOS.md). Full 5-whys for the PERIOD_START bug is also in TODOS.md.
